quote: Pay attention. Be astonished. Tell about it.
through_line: Being present costs something. The week ends with a reminder that the most irreplaceable thing we bring — to our work, our classrooms, our relationships, our practice — is the readiness to be moved.

articles.title: Can artificial intelligence help us become less lonely?
articles.pitch: The loneliness epidemic is real, and AI companions are increasingly marketed as one response. Greater Good examines the evidence honestly — what AI interaction does and doesn’t offer, and the conditions under which it might genuinely help versus substitute for something harder to build.

articles.title: Human Strategy In An AI-Accelerated Workflow
articles.pitch: As AI tools accelerate the pace of design and development work, the question shifts from speed to judgment. This Smashing Magazine piece looks at where human strategy becomes essential in an AI-assisted workflow — and how to hold onto it when the tools encourage you to skip it.

articles.title: A Path to Improving Students’ Emotional Intelligence
articles.pitch: Emotional intelligence — the capacity to recognize and work with feelings in real time — is both a predictor of academic success and a skill that can be explicitly taught. This piece offers practical frameworks teachers can use today, from the Mood Meter to mood-aligned choice boards.

articles.title: Blindsided by Impermanence
articles.pitch: A family separation, Pema Chödrön’s voice, Thich Nhat Hanh’s reminder that impermanence is not a concept but a practice — Mitchell Ratner’s essay is a quietly moving account of being blindsided by loss and what the teachings actually feel like when applied in that moment.

articles.title: Scientists trick the eye into seeing new color ‘olo’
articles.pitch: Using micro-precise laser pulses targeted at individual cone cells in the retina, UC Berkeley scientists made five people see a color that doesn’t exist in nature — a saturated blue-green they named ‘olo.’ A short, wonderful piece about the edge of human perception.
